runtime_preference: MLX for Apple Silicon resident experts
llama.cpp Metal for GGUF compatibility and broad tooling

runtime_policy.reason: The tested 24 GB desktop stays responsive with Qwen3 4B plus Qwen3 1.7B resident. Qwen3 30B remains an isolated quality-first profile because concurrent desktop use caused severe swap pressure.

candidates.purpose: Fast real endpoint smoke test; not a production-quality general model.
Optional quality-first isolated profile for instruction following, reasoning, multilingual work, and tool use. It is not resident beside another model on the tested 24 GB desktop.
Higher ambition general/agentic model; test with capped context because 24 GB headroom is tight.
Rejected on the tested Apple M5 Pro / 24 GiB machine after Metal OOM at both 8192 and 2048 KV cache sizes. Keep as a research candidate only for larger-memory Apple Silicon.
Alternative for chat, reasoning, vision, and broad multimodal general use.
Preferred Gemma multimodal stretch candidate. Keep cold-loaded or single-resident on 24 GB because headroom is tighter than Gemma E4B.

Supported optional fallback for summarization, context compaction, and classification. It requires the pinned MLX profile and is not the 24 GiB resident default because joint Qwen 30B + Gemma live generation caused severe swap pressure.
Default resident general model on 24 GiB Apple Silicon: materially more headroom than Qwen3 30B while retaining broad chat, reasoning, writing, and multilingual coverage.
Memory-bounded resident fallback for summaries, rewrites, extraction, and compaction beside Qwen3 4B on the tested 24 GiB Apple Silicon profile.
Specialist loaded only when coding workflows justify it; not the default for a general-purpose app.

Legacy GGUF specialist for Python/coding tasks. It is not the general-purpose default because the model card positions it as a coding fine-tune, and the same author now publishes a v2 agentic successor.

Preferred successor to the v1 GGUF coding model for local coding, terminal, and tool-use experiments. Still not the default for this general-purpose app until it wins a local eval slice against the Qwen general baseline.
Reject for this machine unless using much lower quality quants or external hardware.
